description:
A developer just noticed that several temporary “.tmp” files are scattered throughout a project and wants them consolidated.  
    1. Current layout  
       • /home/user/my_project/  
         ├── src/old.tmp  
         ├── src/main.c  
         ├── test/data.tmp  
         ├── debug.log  
         └── notes/tmp/info.tmp  
    
    2. Goal  
       • Create a new directory called /home/user/my_project/backup_tmp  
       • Move every “.tmp” file that is currently anywhere inside /home/user/my_project/ **into that new directory**.  
       • Produce a text log named /home/user/tmp_backup.log that lists the absolute, original path of every moved “.tmp” file.  
         – One absolute path per line, no extra spaces or blank lines.  
         – The list must be **sorted in lexicographical ascending order** before being written.  
       • When finished:  
         – /home/user/my_project should have zero “.tmp” files left in their old locations.  
         – /home/user/my_project/backup_tmp should contain the moved *.tmp files (filenames only, no sub-folders).  
         – /home/user/tmp_backup.log must match the exact format described above so the automated grader can compare the file line-by-line.  
    
    3. Constraint  
       • Achieve everything with **one single shell command** (you may use pipelines, “&&”/“;” chains, or grouping, but submit it as a single command).  
    
    4. Verification hints (for you, the AI assistant)  
       • The grader will:  
         1. Confirm that the backup_tmp directory exists.  
         2. Check that old *.tmp paths no longer exist in their original places.  
         3. Ensure the *.tmp files exist in backup_tmp.  
         4. Read /home/user/tmp_backup.log and confirm it contains exactly the correct, sorted, absolute paths—one per line, no trailing spaces, final newline optional.
truth:
<!-- Initial state before agent starts -->
    <initial>
        <directories>
            /home/user/my_project/
            /home/user/my_project/src/
            /home/user/my_project/test/
            /home/user/my_project/notes/
            /home/user/my_project/notes/tmp/
        </directories>
        <files>
            /home/user/my_project/src/old.tmp           (empty file)
            /home/user/my_project/src/main.c            (empty file)
            /home/user/my_project/test/data.tmp         (empty file)
            /home/user/my_project/debug.log             (empty file)
            /home/user/my_project/notes/tmp/info.tmp    (empty file)
        </files>
    </initial>

    <!-- Required end-state for automated grading -->
    <required>
        <directory_created>/home/user/my_project/backup_tmp</directory_created>
        <tmp_files_in_backup_tmp>
            /home/user/my_project/backup_tmp/old.tmp
            /home/user/my_project/backup_tmp/data.tmp
            /home/user/my_project/backup_tmp/info.tmp
        </tmp_files_in_backup_tmp>
        <log_file>/home/user/tmp_backup.log</log_file>
        <log_contents>
            /home/user/my_project/notes/tmp/info.tmp
            /home/user/my_project/src/old.tmp
            /home/user/my_project/test/data.tmp
        </log_contents>
        <no_tmp_remaining_outside_backup>true</no_tmp_remaining_outside_backup>
    </required>
